text:
DEPARTMENT OF PATHOLOGY AND LABORATORY MEDICINE
SURGICAL PATHOLOGY REPORT

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
PATIENT: Chen, Margaret
MRN: [redacted]
DATE OF PROCEDURE: [see header]
DATE OF ACCESSION: [see header]
ACCESSION NUMBER: SP-[redacted]
ORDERING PHYSICIAN: [ENT/Head & Neck Surgery]
ATTENDING PATHOLOGIST: [redacted, MD]
CLINICAL HISTORY: 52-year-old Chinese-American female, never-smoker, presenting with a 3-month history of right-sided throat discomfort and mild odynophagia. Indirect laryngoscopy demonstrated a right base of tongue mass. Rule out malignancy.
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

SPECIMEN SUBMITTED:
A: Right base of tongue, direct laryngoscopy biopsy

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
FINAL DIAGNOSIS:

A. RIGHT BASE OF TONGUE, BIOPSY:
   - SQUAMOUS CELL CARCINOMA, HPV-ASSOCIATED (p16 POSITIVE)
   - Moderately differentiated
   - See comment and ancillary studies below

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
COMMENT:
The histomorphology in combination with the immunohistochemical and molecular findings is consistent with HPV-associated (transcriptionally active HPV-16) squamous cell carcinoma of the oropharynx. The tumor demonstrates the characteristic non-keratinizing to focally keratinizing architecture, pleomorphic nuclei with prominent nucleoli, and basaloid cytology with areas of more abundant eosinophilic cytoplasm, consistent with moderately differentiated HPV-positive OPSCC. Strong and diffuse p16 overexpression (>75% of tumor cells, nuclear and cytoplasmic) is confirmed by immunohistochemistry. HPV RNA in situ hybridization (RNAscope) demonstrates punctate and diffuse nuclear and cytoplasmic signal in tumor cells, confirming the presence of transcriptionally active high-risk HPV. These findings are consistent with the clinical and radiographic presentation of a right base of tongue primary tumor.

Lymphovascular invasion is not identified in this biopsy specimen. Perineural invasion is not identified. Tumor margin assessment is not applicable to this biopsy specimen.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
GROSS DESCRIPTION:

Specimen A is received fresh in a container labeled "right base of tongue biopsy." The specimen consists of multiple tan-pink, somewhat friable, irregular soft tissue fragments. In aggregate, the fragments measure approximately 1.2 x 0.8 x 0.4 cm. The tissue is entirely submitted for histologic examination in two cassettes (A1-A2). No grossly identifiable normal mucosa is present. No necrosis or calcification is identified grossly. The specimen is entirely submitted.

Cassettes: A1 - smaller tissue fragments, entirely submitted; A2 - larger tissue fragment, bisected, entirely submitted.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
MICROSCOPIC DESCRIPTION:

Histologic sections of the right base of tongue biopsy reveal fragments of respiratory-type and stratified squamous epithelium overlying a neoplastic proliferation. The tumor demonstrates an invasive growth pattern with predominantly nested and sheet-like architectural arrangements. Individual tumor cell nests are surrounded by a desmoplastic fibrous stroma with scattered inflammatory infiltrate including lymphocytes and plasma cells.

Tumor cells are polygonal to elongated, with moderate to abundant eosinophilic cytoplasm. Nuclei are enlarged, vesicular, and pleomorphic with prominent single or multiple nucleoli. Mitotic figures are readily identified, including atypical forms. Focal intercellular bridges and small foci of individual cell keratinization are present, though overt keratin pearl formation is not conspicuous. A subset of cells in the periphery of nests displays a basaloid morphology with higher nuclear-to-cytoplasmic ratios, consistent with the spectrum of moderately differentiated HPV-positive SCC.

The overlying surface squamous epithelium shows focal dysplastic changes at the epithelial-stromal interface.

Lymphovascular spaces: No convincing lymphovascular invasion identified on H&E-stained sections.
Perineural invasion: Not identified.
Necrosis: Focal comedonecrosis within tumor nests, not extensive.
Margins: Not applicable (incisional biopsy specimen).

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
ANCILLARY STUDIES:

1. IMMUNOHISTOCHEMISTRY (performed on Block A2):

   p16 (CINtec, clone E6H4; Ventana BenchMark Ultra):
   POSITIVE - strong, diffuse nuclear and cytoplasmic staining in greater than 75% of tumor cells (3+). Staining pattern is continuous and block-type, consistent with HPV-associated overexpression. Internal positive and negative controls satisfactory.

   p40 (clone BC28; Ventana):
   POSITIVE - strong nuclear staining confirming squamous lineage. Staining intensity is diffuse throughout the tumor cells.

   CK5/6 (clone D5/16B4; Dako):
   POSITIVE - strong, diffuse cytoplasmic staining consistent with squamous differentiation.

   Ki-67 (clone MIB-1; Dako):
   Proliferative index approximately 65-70%, reflecting high mitotic activity consistent with invasive carcinoma.

   SOX2 (clone EP118; Cell Marque):
   POSITIVE - diffuse nuclear staining. Consistent with squamous/oropharyngeal phenotype.

2. HPV RNA IN SITU HYBRIDIZATION (RNAscope):
   Probe: High-Risk HPV cocktail (HPV types 16, 18, 26, 31, 33, 35, 39, 45, 51, 52, 56, 58, 59, 66, 68)
   Result: POSITIVE - punctate (episomal) and diffuse (integrated) nuclear and cytoplasmic signal identified in the majority of tumor cells. The pattern is most consistent with HPV-16 (supported by type-specific reflex probing; HPV-16 specific probe POSITIVE). Signal is not present in the overlying non-neoplastic squamous epithelium or stromal cells. Positive and negative controls satisfactory.
   Interpretation: Consistent with transcriptionally active, high-risk HPV (type 16) infection. Findings confirm HPV-driven carcinogenesis.

3. NOTE ON EBV (performed to exclude nasopharyngeal primary):
   EBER ISH: NEGATIVE. No EBV-encoded RNA signal identified in tumor cells. This finding supports a primary oropharyngeal site rather than a nasopharyngeal carcinoma.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
SUMMARY TABLE:

  Histologic Type:                  Squamous cell carcinoma, invasive
  HPV Status (RNA ISH):             POSITIVE (high-risk HPV, type 16)
  p16 IHC:                          POSITIVE (>75% tumor cells, 3+ intensity)
  Grade:                            Moderately differentiated
  Lymphovascular Invasion:          Not identified
  Perineural Invasion:              Not identified
  Tumor Necrosis:                   Focal (comedonecrosis within nests)
  Margin Status:                    Not applicable (biopsy)
  EBV (EBER ISH):                   Negative

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Electronic signature: [Attending Pathologist, MD]
Department of Pathology and Laboratory Medicine
Report finalized: [date redacted]
